title: Inclusive Interaction, Content, Responsive Design, and Human Evaluation · Guided Vertical Slice
summary: A guided product team extends accessible responsive product experience while preserving every earlier problem, accessibility, security, testing, operations, and recovery requirement. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of accessible responsive product experience while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Structure landmarks, headings, names, instructions, controls, errors, and status around the user task and reading order.
Verify complete keyboard operation, logical order, visible focus, focus movement, escape paths, and no traps across state changes.
Design content and interaction to reflow at zoom and changed mobile, tablet, desktop, orientation, text, and language conditions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Semantic Task Flow
steps.instruction:
workshop case — The primary workflow works by mouse at one desktop width but loses reading order, focus, status, error recovery, and meaning under changed access needs. The workshop variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Semantic Task Flow.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for structure landmarks, headings, names, instructions, controls, errors, and status around the user task and reading order. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Semantic Task Flow, keep Semantic Task Flow connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: The primary workflow works by mouse at one desktop width but loses reading order, focus, status, error recovery, and meaning under changed access needs. The workshop variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Semantic Task Flow condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: SPDX specification — Controls machine-readable software, package, file, snippet, licensing, security, build, and AI artifact relationships.

  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

content.text: The Human Accessibility Eval structural check is only one gate. accessible responsive product experience must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Human Accessibility Eval

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits.
